subject: science
title: science_15.json
description: Grade 4 Science Assessment - Day 15: Light and Interaction with Materials

questions.q: Which type of material allows all light rays to pass through completely unhindered, so that you can see through it with perfect clarity?
options: Transparent
Translucent
Opaque
Reflective
questions.hint: Clean glass windows and clear plastic wrap are perfect examples.
questions.explanation: Transparent materials permit light to pass through them with minimal scattering or absorption, allowing distinct images to be seen clearly on the other side.

questions.q: What happens to light rays when they strike a smooth, polished piece of silver metal?
options: They bounce off uniformly in a regular reflection.
They pass straight through without altering direction.
They split apart into a bright rainbow spectrum.
They are completely absorbed and disappear as heat.
questions.answer: They bounce off uniformly in a regular reflection.
questions.hint: Think about what happens to a ball when you bounce it on a smooth garage floor.
questions.explanation: Specular or regular reflection occurs when light hits a highly uniform, smooth surface, causing the rays to bounce off at the exact same angle they hit.

questions.q: What dark area is produced on a wall or floor when an opaque object blocks the path of light rays?
questions.hint: It follows you around on a sunny afternoon and mimics your movements.
questions.explanation: A shadow is formed when light traveling in straight lines is obstructed by an opaque object that cannot transmit light.

questions.q: Why do some underwater divers wear masks containing curved glass lenses that correct how light looks underwater?
options: To adjust for the refraction that occurs when light travels from water into the air pocket of the mask.
To block the high levels of magnetic light generated by coral reefs.
To trap potential energy inside the diver's eye sockets.
To make the water change its physical state from liquid to solid.
questions.answer: To adjust for the refraction that occurs when light travels from water into the air pocket of the mask.
questions.hint: Think about how light bends and changes speed when moving between different materials like water and air.
questions.explanation: Light rays refract (bend) when moving from water into the air space inside the goggles. Specialized curved lenses correct this bending so the diver can see sizes and distances accurately.

questions.q: If you look through a piece of dark green stained glass, what is happening to the other colors of light?
options: The green light passes through, while the other colors are absorbed or blocked.
The glass changes all other colors into green light molecules.
The glass reflects only the red and blue wavelengths into space.
All colors are amplified evenly to make the view brighter.
questions.answer: The green light passes through, while the other colors are absorbed or blocked.
questions.hint: Think about how a filter screens out everything except its own specific color.
questions.explanation: Colored transparent or translucent materials act as filters, transmitting specific wavelengths of light (like green) while absorbing or reflecting the remaining colors of the spectrum.

questions.q: What term describes the property of a material that completely blocks and drinks in light energy rather than letting it pass through?
questions.hint: A black asphalt driveway does this on a hot sunny day, getting very warm.
questions.explanation: Absorption occurs when matter captures light energy and converts it into internal thermal energy instead of reflecting or transmitting it.

questions.q: Which of the following surfaces would create a diffuse or scattered reflection, making it impossible to see your own clear image?
options: A crumpled piece of shiny aluminum foil
A perfectly flat bathroom mirror
A calm, still puddle of rainwater
A polished pane of heavy window glass
questions.answer: A crumpled piece of shiny aluminum foil
questions.hint: Look for the surface that is bumpy, rough, or uneven.
questions.explanation: Diffuse reflection happens when light hits a rough or uneven surface, bouncing the individual rays off in many different directions and scattering the image.

questions.q: What is an object that generates and releases its own visible light called?
options: Luminous
Refractive
Opaque
Absorbent
questions.hint: The Sun, stars, and lit candles are described by this word.
questions.explanation: Luminous objects are primary sources of light that produce and emit their own illumination, unlike non-luminous objects that only reflect light.

questions.q: How does a standard optical magnifying glass help make tiny text appear larger to a reader?
options: The convex glass lens refracts light rays inward, focusing them to enlarge the image.
The lens adds extra chemical matter directly to the paper.
The glass reflects the light backward into the lightbulb.
The tool blocks out all shadow particles on the desk.
questions.answer: The convex glass lens refracts light rays inward, focusing them to enlarge the image.
questions.hint: Think about how a curved piece of glass bends light waves as they pass through it.
questions.explanation: A magnifying glass utilizes a convex lens to refract (bend) light rays converging toward the eye, causing the object to project a larger virtual image on the retina.

questions.q: What color do you get when a prism recombines or blends all the wavelengths of the visible light spectrum back together?
questions.hint: It is the color of normal sunlight before it gets split into a rainbow.
questions.explanation: White light is a combination of all the colors of the visible spectrum mixed together simultaneously.
